concerns.text: FR-007 mandates 'CPU-only hardware' with '≤ 7 GB' memory and '≤ 6 hours' execution. However, the Assumptions section (and the resolved block) explicitly states that a 1.5B parameter model is 'sufficiently small' to run under these constraints. Running a 1.5B model (even quantized) on CPU within 7GB RAM for a full training loop (OPD + RL) is physically contradictory to standard hardware limits for this parameter count, creating a direct conflict between the hardware constraint (FR-007) and the model size assumption.

The Assumptions section contains a malformed, unresolved block at the end: '[RESOLVED: ...] problems contain enough variance...'. This text appears to be a copy-paste error where a resolved assumption was merged with a new, unformatted question about sample size and power analysis. This creates a contradiction in the document state (claiming 'RESOLVED' while asking a question) and breaks the logical flow of the assumptions list.

User Story 3 references 'the direction of the optimal solution (estimated via reference)' in Acceptance Scenario 2. However, the 'Resolved' block in Assumptions explicitly states that a high-accuracy reference model cannot fit in memory and mandates using the 'standard PPO baseline' as a proxy. The terminology in US-3 ('optimal solution') contradicts the defined proxy ('standard PPO baseline') without clarifying that they are being treated as equivalent in this context, causing ambiguity in the acceptance criteria.

FR-005 requires computing cosine similarity against 'the direction of the optimal solution (estimated via a high-accuracy reference model)'. This directly contradicts the 'Resolved' block in Assumptions which states: 'A high-accuracy reference model... cannot fit... The system MUST use the final accumulated update direction of the standard PPO baseline... as the proxy'. The requirement demands an entity (high-accuracy reference model) that the assumptions explicitly forbid using.
